content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


May 30, 1990


Mrs. Isabel Sewell
1049 Noel Drive #1
Menlo Park, CA  94025

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-310

Dear Mrs. Sewell:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

FACTS

You are a member of an organization called The Alliance. The
purpose of The Alliance is to provide a forum for its members to
discuss issues and endorse candidates supportive of the
organization's goals.  In a telephone conversation with me on May
9, 1990 you clarified that there are no membership dues or monies
collected for political purposes and that the only funds collected
are to pay for meeting supplies.  Members of The Alliance may do
door-to-door canvassing for candidates on a volunteer basis.

QUESTION

Does The Alliance meet the Act's definition of a committee?

CONCLUSION

The Alliance does not meet the Act's definition of a
committee.


_________________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

ANALYSIS

Section 82015 defines contribution as:

...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by
a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment
except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes...

***

...Notwithstanding the foregoing definition of
"contribution," the term does not include volunteer personal
services or payments made by an individual for his or her own
travel expense if such payments are made voluntarily without
any understanding or agreement that they shall be, directly
or indirectly, repaid to him or her.

Section 82031 defines independent expenditure as:

...an expenditure made by any person in connection with a
communication which expressly advocates the election or
defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified
measure, or taken as a whole and in context, unambiguously
urges a particular result in an election but which is not
made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or
committee.

An organization qualifies as a "committee" as defined in
Section 82013 if it makes or receives contributions or makes
independent expenditures:

"Committee" means any person or combination of persons
who directly or indirectly does any of the following:
(a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand
dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year.
(b) Makes independent expenditures totaling one or
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year; or
(c) Makes contributions totaling ten thousand dollars
($10,000) or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of
candidates or committees.

According to the facts presented, The Alliance does not make
or receive contributions or make independent expenditures.
Therefore, the activities of The Alliance have not resulted in it
qualifying as a committee under the Act.

In addition, please note that the Act expressly excludes from
the definition of "contribution" the donation of volunteer
personal services to a candidate.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: Does The Alliance meet the Act's definition of a committee?
sections.conclusion: The Alliance does not meet the Act's definition of a committee.
sections.question_synthetic: The questioner asks whether The Alliance, an organization whose members discuss issues and endorse candidates while conducting volunteer canvassing activities, qualifies as a political committee under California campaign finance law.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that The Alliance does not qualify as a committee because it does not make or receive contributions, nor does it make independent expenditures in amounts that would trigger committee status under Government Code Section 82013.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document has clear QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections despite poor OCR quality. Requestor is Mrs. Isabel Sewell; advice letter dated May 30, 1990. Analysis section provides detailed statutory definitions supporting the conclusion.

CONCLUSION: The Alliance does not meet the Act's definition of a committee.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that The Alliance, an organization that allows members to volunteer for candidate campaigns without collecting political funds or making campaign expenditures, does not meet the statutory definition of a political committee under California campaign finance law.
